signals.label: Functional status (real-world)
signals.evidence_span: I still do my garden every morning, the tomatoes are coming in. I cook for myself, I drive to church and the store.

signals.label: Treatment intent
signals.evidence_span: I want the one that gives me the best shot. I didn't drive all this way to go easy.

signals.label: Distance to cellular-therapy center
signals.evidence_span: About an hour and a half, but my daughter drove, so it wasn't bad.

signals.label: Caregiver reliability
signals.evidence_span: I'm Carla, her daughter. I come to the important ones.

signals.label: Gentler vs active preference
signals.evidence_span: Then let's do it. The strong one.

rationale.text: Robust performance status (gardens daily, independent) and explicit active goals argue against under-treating a phenotypically fit patient — age is not the decision.
Patient wants the most active option and is not pursuing CAR-T; a highly active bispecific + chemo combination fits.
Bispecific ramp-up (cycle 1) delivered at the center, then handed back to the local oncologist — makes a distant patient treatable.

off_guideline.boundary: Only epcoritamab monotherapy is approved, and only at 3L+. The GemOx combination and 2L use are off-label.
off_guideline.tradeoff: Off strict guideline for this line, chosen deliberately for a robust, fit, transplant-ineligible patient to avoid under-treatment; grounded in EPCORE NHL-2 (ORR 85%, CR 61%).

rationale.text: A legitimate gentler, chemo-light option for transplant-ineligible disease, but it can under-treat a phenotypically fit patient with primary-refractory disease.
Excluded: primary-refractory disease was an explicit L-MIND exclusion, so there is no efficacy evidence. Not because of age, and not because she is transplant-ineligible.

Excluded on a composite: 90-minute distance from the certified center plus limited caregiver availability make the sustained CRS/ICANS monitoring window unworkable. This is the failing composite — never a numeric age cutoff.

flags.text: Off-label in 2L: only epcoritamab monotherapy is approved (3L+). The GemOx combination and 2L use are off-guideline — grounded in EPCORE NHL-2, name the boundary and proceed with consent.
Bispecific CRS/ICANS risk is front-loaded to cycle-1 step-up: deliver ramp-up at the center for this distant patient, then hand back to the local oncologist.
Evidence-supported and chemo-light, but risks under-treating a phenotypically fit, primary-refractory patient — weigh against her active goals.
